title: Qteq Pty Ltd — Regulatory action
investigation_objective: Queensland-based oil and gas services company fined $5 million by the Federal Court (in proceedings brought by the ACCC) for attempted cartel conduct between 2017-2019, attempting to induce suppliers into arrangements including provisions not to supply services to large oil and gas companies and to rig a multi-million-dollar tender.
scope: Fined $5 million by the Federal Court for attempted cartel conduct (ACCC proceedings); Its executive chairman was separately fined $1 million, described by the ACCC as the highest individual competition-law penalty in Australia
